name: Insulation Tester — Metal Body, Hand-Driven
tagline: Hand-cranked analog insulation tester in anti-magnetic aluminium body — no batteries required. 100 V to 5000 V.
description: The CIE/444 is a hand-driven generator-type insulation tester for direct-reading insulation resistance measurement on electrical machines, rotating plant, switchgear, cables, transformers, and industrial installations. Power is generated entirely by the operator via a collapsible hand crank — no batteries or external power source are required. A centrifugal governor (clutch) holds output voltage constant regardless of crank speed. The instrument features a high-sensitivity cross-coil indicator, anti-parallax mirror scale, and anti-magnetic aluminium body. A guard terminal and externally accessible infinity adjuster are provided on instruments with insulation range 1000 megohms and above. Conforms to ISS: 2992/1987. Quick response: 3 seconds.

specs.label: Generator Type
specs.value: Hand-driven, silent A.C. generator with centrifugal governor (clutch)

applications: Electrical maintenance of rotating plant equipment and stationary apparatus
Insulation control and switching equipment checks
Distribution network and communication cable insulation testing
Transformer and insulator testing; industrial installation checks
Locating intermittent shorts, defective connections, and developing insulation failures

metaTitle: CIE/444 Insulation Tester — Hand-Driven, Metal Body | Cambridge Instruments (CIE), Howrah
metaDesc: CIE/444 hand-driven megger-type insulation tester in aluminium die-cast body. 100 V to 5000 V, battery-free, ISS 2992-1987. 28 voltage/range combinations. Made by Cambridge Instruments & Engg. Co., Howrah.

name: Insulation Tester — Wooden Body, Hand-Driven
tagline: Hand-cranked analog insulation tester in seasoned wooden body — up to 10,000 V. Wider range than metal-body CIE/444.
description: The CIE/666 is a hand-driven generator-type insulation tester built into a seasoned wooden body with sunmica finish, offering a more traditional, robust enclosure. Available in a wider voltage range than the metal-body CIE/444 — from 100 V up to 10,000 V — making it suitable for higher-voltage insulation testing including HV switchgear and higher-class transformer windings. Like the CIE/444, it generates its own test voltage via a hand-cranked A.C. generator with centrifugal governor, eliminating any need for external power. The wooden cabinet has a detachable lid, carrying handle, and lock. Scale length is 100 mm. Quick response: 3 seconds. Conforms to ISS: 2992/1987.

name: Multirange Insulation Tester — Metal Body (Hand-Driven)
tagline: Selectable multi-voltage insulation tester (metal body) with built-in continuity range as standard. Series MR-1 to MR-4.
description: The CIE/444/MR is a multirange version of the hand-driven metal-body CIE/444 insulation tester, with a range selector switch providing multiple rated test voltages on one instrument. Unlike the standard CIE/444, the multirange series includes a continuity range as a standard built-in feature — not a chargeable add-on. Series MR-1 to MR-4 are available in the metal body (CIE/444/MR). Series MR-5 to MR-8 are wooden body (CIE/666/MR) only and are not available in this metal body configuration.

name: Motorised Insulation Tester
tagline: Mains-motor-driven insulation tester for sustained PI and dielectric absorption testing. 500 V to 10,000 V.
description: The CIE/777 is a universal, multi-purpose insulation tester for checking insulation and conductance under simulated operating conditions in high-tension electrical and electronic applications. Driven by a mains motor (230 V AC, 50 Hz), the CIE/777 applies test voltage continuously for as long as needed — enabling genuine polarization index (PI) and dielectric absorption testing that hand-cranked instruments cannot sustain. PI is calculated as resistance after 10 minutes ÷ resistance after 1 minute; better results are obtained with longer periods. Housed in a seasoned wooden cabinet with sunmica finish (detachable lid, handle, lock). Guard ring around the line terminal and guard terminal eliminate surface-leakage errors. Externally accessible infinity adjuster provided. Conforms to IS: 2992-1987. Note on HV testing leads: catalogue lists HV leads as an optional, extra-cost accessory; price list states one pair is supplied with every instrument. Confirm with factory before ordering.

specs.label: Body
specs.value: Seasoned wooden cabinet with sunmica finish, detachable lid, handle, lock

applications: Sustained insulation and polarization index (PI) testing on large rotating plant
Power transformer insulation assessment including dielectric absorption ratio
HV cable and switchgear insulation testing
Substation commissioning by EPC contractors

name: Motor-cum-Hand Driven Insulation Tester
tagline: Motor-cum-hand driven insulation tester — mains motor for PI testing, hand-crank backup for field use. 500 V to 10,000 V.
description: The CIE/777 HM combines mains-motor drive with a collapsible hand-crank backup in one instrument. It shares the same motor-driven sustained-voltage capability as the CIE/777 for polarization index (PI) and dielectric absorption testing, while the centrifugal governor (clutch) on the hand-driven circuit ensures constant output voltage when operating by hand crank where mains power is unavailable. The collapsible generator handle is specific to the 777 HM — it is not present on the plain CIE/777. Weight is 12 kg (including top cover), notably heavier than the 777's 9 kg due to the additional hand-drive mechanism. Housed in the same seasoned wooden cabinet with sunmica finish. Conforms to IS: 2992-1987. Note on HV testing leads: catalogue lists HV leads as optional/extra cost; price list states one pair supplied standard. Confirm with factory.

name: Multirange Motorised Insulation Tester
tagline: Dual-scale motorised insulation tester — inner and outer scale readings cover two ranges per voltage. Series MR-A to MR-E.
description: The CIE/777/MR is a motorised multi-voltage insulation tester with a dual-scale (inner/outer) reading arrangement. Unlike the range-selector-switch approach used in the hand-driven CIE/444/MR and CIE/666/MR, the 777/MR uses two concentric scales on a single movement: the inner scale covers a standard resistance range and the outer scale covers an extended range, at each rated voltage. This lets the operator read either range without switching instruments. Five series (MR-A to MR-E) cover different combinations of rated voltage and inner/outer scale ranges. Retains full sustained-voltage capability (motor-driven) for PI and dielectric absorption testing. Physical specifications (weight, dimensions) for MR-specific variants are not separately confirmed by catalogue — confirm with manufacturer before specifying.

name: Insulation Tester (Hand Driven Generator Type, Bakelite Body)
tagline: A compact, silent hand-driven insulation tester in a high-grade moulded bakelite body — built for direct insulation resistance testing up to 1000 V.
description:
The CIE/555 is a hand-driven generator-type insulation tester housed in a high-grade moulded bakelite body, offering a compact and economical option for direct-reading insulation resistance testing on electrical operating machines, rotating plant equipment, stationary apparatus, control and switching equipment, distribution networks, communication cables, insulators, transformers, and industrial installations.

Like CIE's other hand-driven insulation testers, the CIE/555 uses a hand-driven clutch-type A.C. generator paired with a high-sensitivity cross-coil indicator, with generated A.C. voltage converted to D.C. via silicon diodes. The instrument is dust-free, with rubber shoes holding the surface and giving the unit stability during use, and a collapsible driven handle that folds flush into the casing when not in operation.

The CIE/555 is a versatile instrument for locating intermittent shorts, defective electrical connections, insulation failure, and conductor failure — helping prevent costly repairs and breakdowns. It is suited to testing up to 1000 V, making it well matched to lower-voltage panels, household and light industrial equipment, and general electrical maintenance work.

specs.value: Withstands minimum 2000 V or (Rated Voltage + 1000) V A.C. (rms) between electrical circuit and handle, for 1 minute

specs.label: Insulation Resistance Test
specs.value: Better than 50 MΩ at 500 V between electrical circuit and containing case

Rotating plant equipment and stationary apparatus checks
Insulation control and switching equipment testing
Distribution network testing

Insulator and transformer insulation checks
Industrial installation and wiring checks
Locating intermittent shorts and defective electrical connections

name: Analog Earth Tester — Metal Body
tagline: Hand-driven analog earth tester — 3-terminal (earth resistance) and 4-terminal (earth resistance + soil resistivity). ±5% accuracy per IS: 9223/1979.
description: The CIE/222M is a hand-driven generator-type earth tester for direct measurement of earth resistance in power circuits, telecommunications, railway electrification, domestic and industrial installations, and lightning protection systems. Available in 3-terminal type (earth resistance) and 4-terminal type (earth resistance plus soil resistivity). The 4-terminal type is designed for soil investigation work including geological, agricultural, and civil engineering surveys (tunnels, drainage, sewerage, tube railways). Accuracy ±5% per IS: 9223/1979. A centrifugal governor holds output voltage constant regardless of crank speed. Measurement is unaffected by stray AC currents or back e.m.f. of earth electrolysis.

name: Digital Insulation Tester
tagline: A compact, rechargeable digital insulation tester with direct LCD readout — for fast, accurate insulation resistance measurement on equipment, appliances, and cabling.
description:
The DIT-5005 is a compact, hand-held digital insulation tester with a 3½-digit LCD display, providing direct measurement of insulation resistance for electrical equipment, household appliances, and industrial, commercial, and residential installations. It is housed in a rugged ABS body and includes a facility to be used as a bench-top instrument as well as a hand-held meter.

The instrument is powered by Ni-Cd rechargeable cells, charged via the supplied charging cord, with low-battery indication on the display when recharging is needed. A single push-test button initiates the measurement, with the result read directly off the LCD in megohms.

The DIT-5005 is well suited to insulation testing of motors, transformers, and machines; household appliances such as mixers, toasters, and washing machines; industrial, commercial, and residential electrical installations; and electrical cables used in distribution and communication networks.

name: Digital Insulation Tester + Multimeter — up to 2500 V
tagline: Combined digital insulation tester (100 V–2500 V, 50 GΩ) and True-RMS multimeter with DAR, PI, capacitance, and diode test. CAT III 1000 V / CAT IV 600 V.
description: The DIT-2500 combines a digital insulation resistance tester with a full-function True-RMS digital multimeter in one handheld instrument. Insulation testing at five selectable voltages (100 V to 2500 V) with resistance measurement to 50 GΩ, including Dielectric Absorption Ratio (DAR) and Polarization Index (PI) calculations. Also functions as a general-purpose multimeter: AC/DC voltage up to 1500 V, resistance to 60 MΩ, capacitance to 100 mF, continuity, diode test, and ACV-Hz frequency measurement. Powered by rechargeable Li-ion battery (3.7 V × 2). Safety rated CAT III 1000 V / CAT IV 600 V. Features data hold, backlight, and auto power-off. Dimensions: 189 × 89 × 55 mm. Weight: approx. 380 g. Standard accessories (test leads, charging cable) should be confirmed with manufacturer — not separately documented in the current source.

name: Digital Insulation Tester — up to 5000 V / 200 GΩ
tagline: Auto-ranging digital insulation tester to 5000 V with HV LED indicator, auto zero calibration, and large-character LCD. 6 × AA batteries.
description: The DIT-5000 is a dedicated digital insulation tester testing insulation resistance at voltages up to 5000 V, with resistance measurement up to 200 GΩ. Auto-range operation, automatic zero calibration, and automatic polarity display simplify field use. A high-voltage LED indicator warns the operator when the output is live. Results are shown on a 3½-digit LCD (68 × 55 mm, 35 mm character height) with backlight. Also includes AC voltage measurement up to 750 V (secondary function — not a full multimeter). Powered by 6 × 1.5 V AA batteries (replaceable, not rechargeable). Accuracy is tiered by resistance range (see specifications). Dimensions: 218 × 122 × 75 mm. Weight: approx. 900 g. Supplied with test leads, batteries, user manual, and carrying box.

name: Digital Earth Resistance Tester
tagline: Rugged ABS-bodied digital earth resistance tester — 4-terminal, rechargeable Ni-Cd battery. Dual and triple range.
description: The DET-2000 is a digital earth resistance tester measuring earth resistance and soil resistivity, with results shown on a 3½-digit LCD display. Four terminals (E1, P1, P2, E2) allow 3-terminal earth-resistance testing or 4-terminal soil-resistivity measurement. Housed in a rugged ABS body, hand-held with facility to convert to bench-top use. Rechargeable Ni-Cd cells are charged via the supplied charging cord (230 V, 50 Hz). Lo-Bat indicator on display. Accuracy: ±3% for 0–10% and 90–100% of range; ±1.5% for 10–90% of range (per IS: 9223/1979). Weight: 575 g. Dimensions: 172 × 98 × 38 mm. Note: the testing kit (spikes, wire, hammer) is an optional, separately priced accessory — it is not supplied standard.

name: Digital Micro-Ohm Meter (4-Terminal Kelvin)
tagline: Bench-top digital micro-ohm meter with 4-terminal (Kelvin) measurement — 1999 µΩ to 19.99 kΩ across 8 ranges. 7-segment LED display.
description: The MR-253A (formerly catalogued as MR-253) is a compact, precision bench-top micro-ohm meter for measuring low values of resistance — in cables, transformer and motor windings, switch and relay contact resistance, PCB track resistance, and ammeter shunt wire calibration. It uses a four-terminal (Kelvin) measurement technique that eliminates errors from lead and contact resistance, which is critical for accurate measurement in the microohm-to-kiloohm range. Displayed on a 3½-digit, 1999-count seven-segment LED display. Test current varies automatically by range, from 1 A (lowest resistance ranges) down to 0.01 mA (highest range). Accuracy: ±0.5% ±2 digits. Built using MOS LSI technology. Mains powered, 230 V, 50 Hz. Dimensions: 192 × 96 × 230 mm. Weight: 2.25 kg. Supplied with 2 pairs of test leads.

specs.label: Measurement Technique
specs.value: 4-terminal (Kelvin) — eliminates lead and contact resistance error

specs.label: Test Current
specs.value: Automatically varies by range: 1 A (µΩ ranges) down to 0.01 mA (kΩ range)

applications: Cable and wire resistance measurement
Transformer and motor winding resistance checks
Switch and relay contact resistance testing
PCB track resistance testing and ammeter shunt calibration

name: Digital Clamp Multimeter — True-RMS AC, 1000 A
tagline: True-RMS AC clamp multimeter — 6 A/60 A/600 A/1000 A, NCV, Low-Z ACV, 6000-count. AC current only (no DC current).
description: The DCM 2250 TR is a True-RMS digital clamp multimeter measuring AC current up to 1000 A across four ranges (6 A, 60 A, 600 A, 1000 A), along with AC/DC voltage, low-impedance ACV (Low Z), and resistance. This model measures AC current only — DC current measurement is not available on the DCM 2250 TR (see DCM 5410 TR for AC/DC current capability). Features include non-contact voltage (NCV) and live-wire test, 6000-count auto-ranging display, True-RMS measurement, data hold, MIN/MAX, backlight, torch, and auto power-off. Low-impedance ACV mode avoids false 'ghost voltage' readings on unloaded circuits. 40 mm jaw opening. Safety rated CAT III 600 V. Powered by 2 × AAA batteries. Weight: approx. 315 g.

specs.label: DC Current
specs.value: Not available — AC current only on this model

specs.label: Low Impedance ACV
specs.value: 6 V / 60 V / 300 V — input impedance ≤3 KΩ

applications: AC current measurement on panels and distribution boards
Electrical maintenance and switchgear testing
Cable and circuit continuity and live-wire checks

name: Digital Clamp Multimeter — True-RMS AC/DC, 1000 A
tagline: True-RMS AC/DC clamp multimeter — 1000 A AC and DC, plus capacitance, frequency, and temperature. CAT III 600 V.
description: The DCM 5410 TR is a True-RMS digital clamp multimeter measuring both AC and DC current up to 1000 A, along with AC/DC voltage, resistance, capacitance, frequency (10 Hz–10 MHz), and temperature (−20°C to 1000°C). The DC current clamp capability is the key differentiator from the AC-only DCM 2250 TR, making the DCM 5410 TR suitable for solar, battery, and DC distribution work. Features: 6000-count auto-ranging display, NCV and live-wire test, Low-Z ACV mode, data hold, MIN/MAX, backlight, torch, and auto power-off. 40 mm jaw opening. Safety rated CAT III 600 V. Powered by 2 × AAA batteries. Weight: approx. 315 g.

specs.label: Low Impedance ACV
specs.value: 6 V / 60 V / 300 V — input impedance ≤3 KΩ

applications: AC and DC current measurement on panels and distribution boards
Solar and battery system diagnostics (DC current clamp)
Electrical maintenance, switchgear, and panel testing
Combined electrical and temperature diagnostics

name: Digital Multimeter — 3½-digit
tagline: 3½-digit DMM — 1000 V DC, 750 V AC, 20 A, resistance, capacitance, continuity, diode, hFE.
description: The DM 235 is a 3½-digit digital multimeter measuring DC voltage up to 1000 V, AC voltage, and resistance — a compact, general-purpose instrument for everyday electrical measurement in industrial and commercial environments. It provides straightforward operation for voltage checks, resistance measurements, and continuity testing. The DM 235 is suited to electricians, electrical contractors, panel builders, and maintenance engineers who need a reliable, cost-effective multimeter for routine field measurements. Supplied by Cambridge Instruments & Engg. Co., Howrah.
applications: General electrical measurement
Panel testing
Workshop and field use
metaTitle: DM 235 Digital Multimeter — 3½-digit | Cambridge Instruments (CIE), Howrah
metaDesc: CIE DM 235 3½-digit digital multimeter: DC voltage to 1000 V, AC voltage, resistance, continuity. Compact, general-purpose T&M instrument. Cambridge Instruments & Engg. Co., Howrah, India.

name: True-RMS Digital Multimeter — Manual Ranging, 3½-digit
tagline: True-RMS, manual-ranging 3½-digit DMM with transistor hFE test, capacitance, backlight, and protective holster.
description: The DM 321T is a 3½-digit True-RMS digital multimeter for general electrical and electronic test work. It is a manual-ranging instrument (not auto-ranging), measuring AC/DC voltage, AC/DC current, resistance, and capacitance, alongside continuity test, diode test, and transistor (hFE) test. Supplied with a protective holster. True-RMS measurement gives accurate readings on non-sinusoidal AC waveforms — useful when working on circuits with switching loads or harmonics. DC voltage up to 1000 V; DC current up to 20 A. Auto power-off and data hold standard. Dimensions: 175 × 93 × 55 mm. Weight: approx. 400 g (incl. battery).

specs.label: Ranging
specs.value: Manual (not auto-ranging) — supplied with protective holster

specs.label: Additional Functions
specs.value: Continuity test, diode test, data hold, auto power-off

applications: General electrical maintenance and panel testing
Accurate AC measurement on non-sinusoidal loads (True-RMS)
Basic electronic troubleshooting including transistor and component testing

name: A.C. High Voltage Breakdown Test Set
tagline: Robust powder-coated AC HV breakdown/withstand test set — 0–3 kV or 0–5 kV, 25/50 mA selectable. Analog moving-iron metering, memory device.
description: The CIE/5050A is an A.C. high-voltage breakdown test set for fast, accurate withstand/breakdown testing of electrical and electronic materials, components, equipment, and panel boards. Housed in a robust powder-coated M.S. cabinet with carrying handles. The cast-resin H.V. transformer is specially designed to withstand frequent sparkover and momentary short-circuit conditions inherent in breakdown testing. A continuously variable autotransformer provides smooth output voltage adjustment from zero; zero-start interlocking prevents energising the H.V. circuit unless the regulator is at zero. Tripping current is toggle-switch selectable (25 mA or 50 mA) as standard. A memory device with push-button switch retains the breakdown voltage reading on the meter even after the H.V. circuit trips off. Metering is analog moving-iron (kV meter + milliammeter) per IS 1248:1983 — not digital. Available in two output variants: 0–3 kV and 0–5 kV. One pair of H.V. probe with cable supplied standard.

specs.label: Tripping Current
specs.value: 25 mA & 50 mA — toggle switch selectable (standard feature)

specs.label: Metering
specs.value: Analog moving-iron — KV meter and milliammeter, L.T. side, per IS 1248:1983 (not digital)

specs.label: Memory Device
specs.value: Push-button switch retains breakdown voltage reading after H.V. trip

applications: Breakdown/withstand testing of electrical and electronic components and materials
Equipment and panel board dielectric strength testing
HV withstand testing in production and site environments

includedWith: One pair of H.V. probe with cable
options: Tripping current set point other than standard 25 mA/50 mA — at extra cost

metaTitle: CIE/5050A AC High Voltage Test Set — 0–5 kV | Cambridge Instruments (CIE), Howrah
metaDesc: CIE/5050A AC HV test set: 0–5 kV continuously variable output, analog voltmeter, toggle trip. For HV cable and equipment insulation withstand (hipot) testing. Cambridge Instruments & Engg. Co., Howrah.

name: Phase Sequence Indicator — Disc Rotating Type
tagline: Disc-rotating phase sequence indicator, bakelite body, 100–500 V AC.
description: The PSI-1 is a disc-rotating phase sequence indicator in a bakelite body. The rotating disc physically shows the direction of phase rotation (RYB or RBY), giving an immediate visual indication that is easy to interpret even in bright field lighting. It is used before connecting or energising any 3-phase motor, generator, or transformer — verifying correct phase sequence prevents reverse rotation, mechanical damage, and safety hazards. The PSI-1 connects to the 3-phase supply via three colour-coded test leads. Compact and lightweight, it is a standard tool for electrical commissioning engineers, electricians, and industrial maintenance teams.

specs.label: Accessories
specs.value: R/Y/B test leads and carrying case (supplied)

applications: 3-phase supply phase sequence verification
Motor connection commissioning
Panel and switchboard testing
metaTitle: PSI-1 Phase Sequence Indicator — Disc Rotating Type | Cambridge Instruments (CIE), Howrah
metaDesc: CIE PSI-1 disc-rotating phase sequence indicator: visually shows RYB or RBY rotation before energising motors or generators. Bakelite body. Cambridge Instruments & Engg. Co., Howrah.

name: Phase Sequence Indicator — ABS Moulded
tagline: ABS moulded 96×96 mm phase sequence indicator, 100–500 V. Supplied with case and leads.
description: The PSI-2 is a phase sequence indicator in a compact ABS moulded enclosure (96×96×45 mm), suitable for panel mounting or handheld use. It verifies the phase rotation (RYB or RBY) of a 3-phase AC supply before motors, generators, or other rotating equipment are energised — incorrect phase sequence causes reverse motor rotation, which can damage connected machinery or create safety hazards. The PSI-2 is more compact than the disc-rotating PSI-1 and is preferred where a smaller form factor is needed. Used by commissioning engineers, electrical contractors, panel builders, and industrial maintenance teams across India.
applications: 3-phase phase sequence check
Switchboard commissioning
Motor connection verification
metaTitle: PSI-2 Phase Sequence Indicator — Compact ABS Body | Cambridge Instruments (CIE), Howrah
metaDesc: CIE PSI-2 compact ABS moulded phase sequence indicator (96×96×45 mm). Verifies 3-phase supply rotation before energising motors or generators. Cambridge Instruments & Engg. Co., Howrah.

name: Transformer Oil BDV Tester — 50 kV
tagline: Transformer oil dielectric strength (BDV) tester up to 50 kV. Manual and motorised variants.
description: The CIE/7050 is part of the CIE/7000 Series oil dielectric strength test set range, built for breakdown/withstand voltage testing of transformer and circuit breaker insulating oil per IS 6792:1992. The cast-resin H.V. transformer features graded insulation with the centre point of the H.V. winding grounded, specially designed to withstand frequent sparkover events. A continuously variable autotransformer raises output from zero; zero-start interlocking prevents energising the H.V. circuit unless the regulator is at zero; an interlocked H.T. door cutout means the set cannot be energised with the test chamber open. A memory device retains the breakdown voltage reading after automatic tripping. Metering is analog (96 mm² moving-iron voltmeter, L.T. side, scaled in kV) — digital metering is available at extra cost. Test vessel (oil cell with two brass spheres, 12.5–13 mm diameter, 4 mm or 2.5 mm adjustable gap per IS 6792:1992) is supplied as standard. Available in manual and motorised (MOT) variants; motorised sets advance voltage at approx. 2 kV/second. Note: 75 kV and 80 kV tiers are confirmed by price list but not by the catalogue PDF — confirm construction details with manufacturer before specifying those tiers.

specs.label: H.V. Winding
specs.value: Cast-resin, graded insulation, centre point grounded

specs.label: Metering
specs.value: 96 mm² A.C. moving-iron voltmeter, L.T. side, scaled in kV (analog — not digital standard)

specs.label: Test Vessel
specs.value: Non-absorbent insulating material; two brass spheres 12.5–13 mm dia.; gap 4 mm or 2.5 mm adjustable; per IS 6792:1992

applications: Transformer oil dielectric strength testing before energisation
Routine oil quality monitoring for distribution and power transformers
Circuit breaker oil testing
Power utility and substation maintenance laboratories

name: Relay Test Set — Single Phase (Secondary Current Injection)
tagline: Single-phase relay test set — 0–200 A AC current (14 V/4 V), 0–250 V AC, built-in digital timer. Max 400 VA.
description: The CIE/RTS-5 is a secondary current injection relay test set for testing protective, over-current, and earth fault relays. Provides continuously variable AC current from 0–1 A to 0–200 A across eight ranges, at output voltages of 14 V (for lower current ranges) or 4 V (higher ranges). Alternatively, a 0–250 V AC output at 2 A is available via a Volt/Current selection switch — current injection and 0–250 V AC modes cannot be used simultaneously. Two fixed voltage outputs (50 V and 110 V AC at 2 A) are also provided. A built-in dual-range digital timer (0–9.999 sec at 1 ms resolution or 0–99.99 sec at 10 ms resolution) starts when the output circuit is energised and stops automatically when the relay under test actuates. Multi-range digital ammeter (0–100% scaled) and AC voltmeter (0–300 V) built in. NO/NC selector for relay auxiliary contacts. Input: 230 V, 1 phase, 50 Hz. Max burden: 400 VA. Intermittent duty: 5 min ON, 10 min OFF.

specs.label: Output Voltage (alternative)
specs.value: 0–250 V A.C. at 2 A (mutually exclusive with current output)

specs.label: Timer
specs.value: 9.999 sec (1 ms resolution) or 99.99 sec (10 ms resolution)

applications: Protective and over-current relay testing
Earth fault relay testing
Relay commissioning and maintenance in substations and panels
metaTitle: RTS-5 Single-Phase Relay Test Set, 0–200 A, 400 VA | Cambridge Instruments (CIE), Howrah
metaDesc: RTS-5 single-phase relay test set: 0–200 A AC across ranges, digital timer (0–99.99 s), digital ammeter and voltmeter, 400 VA. For overcurrent and earth fault relays. Cambridge Instruments, Howrah.

name: Relay Test Set — Single Phase, 1000 VA (Portable Trolley)
tagline: Portable trolley-style single-phase relay test set — 0–200 A AC (6 V/50 V), AC or DC voltage output, dual-range timer. Max 1000 VA.
description: The CIE/RTS-5A is a portable secondary current injection relay test set in a single trolley-style unit (top-hinged lid), for testing protective relays, earth fault relays, circuit breakers, and tripping coils in both laboratory and field. Current output is continuously variable across seven ranges (0–1 A to 0–200 A) at 6 V or 50 V for lower ranges (0–1 A to 0–20 A), or 6 V for higher ranges (0–50 A to 0–200 A), via a multi-ratio wound primary current transformer. An AC/DC changeover switch selects either a continuously variable AC voltage output (0–230 V, 240 VA) or a DC voltage output (0–240 V, 240 VA) — these are alternatives, not simultaneous. Digital timer (0–9.999 sec or 0–99.99 sec, accuracy ±1%) with NO/NC auxiliary contact selection and automatic tripping of current output on relay actuation or overload. Max burden: 1000 VA. Supplied with operation manual, schematic, test report, and guarantee certificate.

specs.value: 0–1/5/10/20 A at 6 V or 50 V; 0–50/100/200 A at 6 V — continuously variable

metaDesc: RTS-5A single-phase relay test set on portable trolley: 1000 VA, 0–200 A, digital timer, ammeter and voltmeter. Heavy-duty portable unit for site commissioning. Cambridge Instruments & Engg. Co., Howrah.

name: Three-Phase Relay Test Set
tagline: Three-phase, two-unit relay test set — 0–360° phase angle, 0–50 A current, 415 V 3-phase input. For distance, directional, and differential relay testing.
description: The CIE/RTS-33 is a three-phase relay test set for testing distance relays, over-current relays, over/under-voltage relays, earth fault relays, and directional-element relays. It is a two-unit system: Unit 1 houses the current/voltage source and measuring facilities; Unit 2 is the dedicated phase-shifter unit. Together they allow full 0–360° phase angle control between voltage and current (coarse 60° steps + fine ±30°), enabling genuine directional and distance relay testing not possible with single-phase sets. Three-phase input: 415 V ±10%, 4-wire, 50 Hz. Test current: 0–1/5/10/25/50 A (five taps, 0–100% regulation each). Continuous duty up to 10 A; 25 A and 50 A intermittent. Test voltage: 0–110% of 110 V phase-to-phase, three-phase, 50 Hz. Fixed DC voltage: isolated 30/110/220 V at 100 VA for 30 minutes. Phase-to-phase fault mode: selected phase currents automatically placed in opposition. Facility to check phase sequence before testing. Built-in digital timer (0–9.999 sec and 0–99.99 sec). Rated burden: 250 VA (current and voltage circuits). Units connected by 1-metre interconnection cable.

specs.label: Test Current Taps
specs.value: 0–1 A / 0–5 A / 0–10 A / 0–25 A / 0–50 A (0–100% regulation per tap)

specs.label: Duty
specs.value: Continuous up to 10 A; 25 A & 50 A: intermittent (5 min ON, 10 min OFF)

specs.label: Test Voltage
specs.value: 0–110% of 110 V ph-ph (63.5 V ph-N), 3-phase, 50 Hz, continuously variable

specs.label: Fixed DC Voltage
specs.value: Isolated 30 V / 110 V / 220 V at 100 VA, available for 30 min

specs.label: Time Measurement
